title: raoulia eximia
intro: Raoulia eximia, also known as the "Pig's Ears" or "Raoulia", is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Raoulia eximia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Raoulia eximia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine.
